bird.name: 快速鸚鵡
bird.summary: 红尾绿鹦鹉（學名：Lathamus discolor，英語：Swift Parrot，台湾作快速鸚鵡），又稱雨燕鸚鵡，是鸚形目舊世界鸚鵡科快速鸚鵡屬下的唯一一種鳥類。這個物種棲息於澳洲東南部的桉樹林地區，1月—5月間會遷徙至澳洲本土越冬，其餘時間則在塔斯馬尼亞度過，少數個體為全…

locations.name: 塔斯馬尼亞州
locations.summary: 塔斯馬尼亞州（Tasmania，發音：/tæzˈmeɪniə/；帕拉瓦卡尼語：Lutruwita），簡稱塔州，是澳大利亞唯一的島嶼州，原名范迪門地（Van Diemen's Land，又譯萬迪門蘭）。該州位於澳大利亞大陸東南方約240公里…

locations.name: Warialda
locations.summary: Warialda is a town in the North West Slopes region of New South Wales, Australia, in Gwydir Shire.

locations.name: 新南威爾士州
locations.summary: 新南威爾斯州（英語：New South Wales，缩写为NSW），或譯紐修威省，简称新州、紐省，是澳洲人口最多的一州；位於澳洲的東南部，昆士兰州以南、维多利亞州以北，南澳州以東，首府在悉尼，境內包圍整個澳洲首都領地。新南威爾斯今劃分為1…

locations.name: 阿德莱德
locations.summary: 阿德莱德（英語：Adelaide，/ˈædɪleɪd/；考尔纳语：Tarndanya，發音：[ˈd̪̥aɳɖaɲa]）是澳大利亚聯邦南澳大利亚州的首府和也是该州第一大城、商业和文化中心，澳大利亚第五大城，人口逾141万。77%的南澳洲居民…

locations.name: 森林
locations.summary: 森林，是一个高密度树木的区域（或历史上，森林是一个为狩猎而留出的荒地），涵蓋大約9.4％的地球表面（或佔約30％的總土地面積）。聯合國糧食及農業組織(FAO)將森林定義為「面積超過 0.5 公頃、樹木超過 5 公尺且樹冠覆蓋率超過 10%…

relations.subject: 快速鸚鵡
relations.evidence: |last3=Stojanovic |first3=Dejan |date=2024 |title=Influence of molecular marker type on estimating effective population size and other genetic parameters in a critically endangered parrot |journal=Ecology and Evolution |language=en |volume=14 |issue=3 |article-number=e11102 |doi=10.1002/ece3.11102 |pmid=38524913 |issn=2045-7758 |pmc=10961163|bibcode=2024EcoEv..1411102O }} This supports the results of earlier studies that use demographic information about swift parrots to predict that the species could be extinct by 2031. Habitat for the critically endangered swift parrot is being "knowingly destroyed" by logging because of government failures to manage the species' survival.{{Cite journal |last1=Webb |first1=Matthew H
relations.object_summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

relations.subject: 快速鸚鵡
relations.evidence: Birds Australia, Melbourne Logging has already had severe impacts on habitat availability in recent decades and there is evidence that up to 23% of swift parrot breeding habitat has been logged just in the Southern Forests region of Tasmania over the last 20 years. Deforestation also affects the rate of predation by sugar gliders – where mature forest cover is diminished, parrots suffer worse predation rates. Sugar glider predation is worst where logging is severe; these threats interact in a synergistic manner. Given the severity of deforestation across the breeding range, and the relationship between deforestation and sugar glider predation intensity, habitat loss in critical breeding areas of Tasmania may be the species most severe threat
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
